ddls:
CREATE TABLE "districts" (
  "district_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each district */,
  "district_name" TEXT /* Name of the district */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the district */,
  "latitude" TEXT /* Latitude of the district */,
  "longitude" TEXT /* Longitude of the district */,
  "population" INTEGER /* Population of the district */,
  "area_sq_miles" TEXT /* Area of the district in square miles */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("district_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "offenses" (
  "offense_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each offense */,
  "offense_name" TEXT /* Name of the offense */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the offense */,
  "category" TEXT /* Category of the offense (e.g., violent, property) */,
  "severity_level" TEXT /* Severity level of the offense (e.g., low, medium, high) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("offense_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "methods" (
  "method_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each method */,
  "method_name" TEXT /* Name of the method */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the method */,
  "category" TEXT /* Category of the method (e.g., physical, digital) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("method_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "shifts" (
  "shift_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each shift */,
  "shift_name" TEXT /* Name of the shift */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the shift */,
  "start_time" TEXT /* Start time of the shift */,
  "end_time" TEXT /* End time of the shift */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("shift_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., officer, analyst, admin) */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department of the user (e.g., police, detective) */,
  "badge_number" TEXT /* Badge number of the user (if applicable) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "crimes" (
  "crime_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each crime report */,
  "year" INTEGER /* Year the crime was reported */,
  "month" TEXT /* Month the crime was reported */,
  "week" INTEGER /* Week of the year the crime was reported */,
  "hour" TEXT /* Hour of the day the crime was reported */,
  "report_date" TEXT /* Date the crime was reported */,
  "shift_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the shift during which the crime was reported */,
  "offense_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the type of offense */,
  "method_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the method used in the crime */,
  "district_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the district where the crime occurred */,
  "incident_description" TEXT /* Description of the incident */,
  "victim_count" INTEGER /* Number of victims involved */,
  "injury_count" INTEGER /* Number of injuries reported */,
  "arrest_made" INTEGER /* Whether an arrest was made */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("crime_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_crimes_shift_id FOREIGN KEY ("shift_id") REFERENCES shifts ("shift_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_crimes_offense_id FOREIGN KEY ("offense_id") REFERENCES offenses ("offense_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_crimes_method_id FOREIGN KEY ("method_id") REFERENCES methods ("method_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_crimes_district_id FOREIGN KEY ("district_id") REFERENCES districts ("district_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "crime_updates" (
  "update_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each update */,
  "crime_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the crime report being updated */,
  "update_date" TEXT /* Date the update was made */,
  "update_description" TEXT /* Description of the update */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the user making the update */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("update_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_crime_updates_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_crime_updates_crime_id FOREIGN KEY ("crime_id") REFERENCES crimes ("crime_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "crime_files" (
  "file_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each file */,
  "crime_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the crime report associated with the file */,
  "file_name" TEXT /* Name of the file */,
  "file_type" TEXT /* Type of the file (e.g., image, document) */,
  "file_date" TEXT /* Date the file was added */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("file_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_crime_files_crime_id FOREIGN KEY ("crime_id") REFERENCES crimes ("crime_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "crime_links" (
  "link_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each link */,
  "crime_id_1" INTEGER /* Reference to the first crime report */,
  "crime_id_2" INTEGER /* Reference to the second crime report */,
  "link_type" TEXT /* Type of link (e.g., related, duplicate) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("link_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_crime_links_crime_id_2 FOREIGN KEY ("crime_id_2") REFERENCES crimes ("crime_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_crime_links_crime_id_1 FOREIGN KEY ("crime_id_1") REFERENCES crimes ("crime_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_roles" (
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each role */,
  "role_name" TEXT /* Name of the role */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the role */,
  "permission_level" TEXT /* Permission level of the role (e.g., admin, read-only) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_role_assignments" (
  "assignment_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each assignment */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the user */,
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the role */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("assignment_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_role_assignments_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_role_assignments_role_id FOREIGN KEY ("role_id") REFERENCES user_roles ("role_id")
)
